To solve problems involving elastic springs and strings:

use F = ma to obtain w
use information given in the question to obtain the amplitude.
smooth HORIZONTAL surface attached to one end of an elastic SPRING:
smooth HORIZONTAL surface attached to one end of an elastic STRING:
move with S.H.M. while the string is taut
move with constant speed while the string is slack
HANGING in equilibrium attached to one end of an elastic SPRING and displaced VERTICAL from its equilibrium position:
the centre of the oscillation will be the equilibrium position.
HANGING in equilibrium attached to one end of an elastic STRING and displaced VERTICAL from its equilibrium position:
move with S.H.M. while the string is taut
perform complete oscillations if the amplitude is no greater than the equilibrium extension
if the amplitude is greater than the equilibrium extension the particle will move freely under gravity while the string is slack
